title: Coconut & Lime Fish Curry
description: My Thermomix take on a Neil Perry-style coconut fish curry — light, zingy and the fish stays whole.
intro: This is my independent Thermomix homage to Neil Perry and the comforting appeal of fish curry. It keeps the spirit of the original inspiration, but the method is written fresh for TM6 and TM7 cooks who want clear settings and a warm, achievable result.
ingredients: 600 g firm white fish, in chunks
steps: Chop onion, garlic and ginger 4 sec / speed 5. Add oil and curry paste, sauté 3 min / 120°C / speed 1.
Add coconut milk, stock, fish sauce. Cook 6 min / 100°C / speed 2.
Add the fish and cook 8 min / 90°C / Reverse / speed 1 — gentle, so it stays whole. Finish with lime and coriander.
tips: Low 90°C on Reverse keeps the fish in beautiful pieces.
Add green beans with the fish for crunch.

faq.a: A firm white fish like snapper, ling or barramundi.

faq.q: Is this the original Neil Perry recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Neil Perry and fish curry; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
